items.question: Write the softmax formula and state two key properties of the output vector π.
items.answer: π_k = exp(z_k) / Σ_j exp(z_j). Properties: all π_k > 0, and Σ_k π_k = 1 (valid Categorical PMF). Argmax of z equals argmax of π.

items.question: Explain the log-sum-exp trick and why it is needed.
items.answer: Compute log Σ exp(z_j) as m + log Σ exp(z_j − m) where m = max(z). Large z causes exp(z) to overflow; shifting by m keeps exponents ≤ 0 so exp stays finite while the result is mathematically exact.

items.question: Why should CrossEntropyLoss receive logits, not softmax probabilities?
items.answer: CrossEntropyLoss applies log_softmax internally. Feeding probabilities double-applies softmax/log, giving wrong loss and gradients. Pass raw logits from the final linear layer.

items.question: How does softmax relate to categorical cross-entropy for one-hot label y?
items.answer: Softmax defines Q with π_k = softmax(z)_k. One-hot P gives H(P,Q) = −log π_y. Training minimizes −log π_y over the dataset — see Cross-entropy for the full H(P, Q) derivation.

items.question: When would you use sigmoid instead of softmax?
items.answer: Binary classification (one sigmoid) or multi-label problems where labels are independent. Softmax is for single-label multi-class where exactly one of K classes is correct and probabilities must sum to 1.
